module: Module 5 — CIS Ubuntu 22.04 LTS Server-Specific Controls
compliant_and_na_checks: 2.1.20 — Ensure X window server services are not in use (PASS)
2.1.1 — Ensure autofs services are not in use (PASS)
2.1.2 — Ensure avahi daemon services are not in use (PASS)
2.1.11 — Ensure print server services are not in use (PASS)
1.1.2.4.2 — Ensure nodev option set on /var partition (N/A)
1.1.2.4.3 — Ensure nosuid option set on /var partition (N/A)
1.1.2.5.2-4 — Ensure nodev, nosuid, noexec options set on /var/tmp partition (N/A)
1.1.2.6.2-4 — Ensure nodev, nosuid, noexec options set on /var/log partition (N/A)
1.1.2.7.2-4 — Ensure nodev, nosuid, noexec options set on /var/log/audit partition (N/A)

tier_1.title: Ensure wireless interfaces are not available
tier_1.message:
Wireless module(s) failed checks:
  module 'rtw88_8821ce': not install-denied (modprobe -n -v shows no /bin/false entry); currently loaded in kernel (lsmod); not deny-listed in modprobe config
tier_1.vulnerability: An active wireless interface on a server creates an unintended RF attack surface. Wireless kernel drivers have a long CVE history including remote code execution. Servers live on wired LANs — wireless hardware must be disabled at the kernel module level.

tier_1.title: Ensure usb-storage kernel module is not available
tier_1.message: module usb_storage is currently loaded (lsmod) | 'install usb_storage /bin/false' not found in modprobe config | 'blacklist usb_storage' not found in modprobe config
tier_1.vulnerability: USB storage is the most common physical vector for introducing malware and exfiltrating data from servers. No USB mass-storage use case exists in a datacenter environment.
